prompt:
I just modified handleBackground() and handleForeground() in VideoPlayerViewModel.swift to fix a bug where the video player resumed playback after the app returned from background, even when the user had paused it. My fix adds a wasPlayingBeforeBackground flag that handleForeground() checks before calling player.play(). Audit my change for any side effects or regressions — is this fix actually complete?

File is at /Users/j.lesouef/.claude/skills/regression-check-workspace/iteration-1/eval-kvo-observer-bypass/fixture/VideoPlayerViewModel.swift

assertions.text: Output identifies the KVO observer at lines ~155-167 as the root cause, flagging case .readyToPlay: player.play() as a BLOCKER or RISK that bypasses the wasPlayingBeforeBackground guard
Output uses the BLOCKER / RISK / NICE-TO-CHECK severity structure from the skill
Output explains *why* the observer fires on return from background (item re-stabilises, status transitions back to .readyToPlay)
Output does NOT just praise the fix as complete — it identifies the change as insufficient
Output cites specific file paths and line numbers in findings
